_description: Site-wide index of all papers in the RQM Technologies technical paper series.

papers.title: Foundations of Quaternionic Quantum Computing: Qubits, Spinors, and SU(2) Geometry
papers.abstract: We develop the foundational framework for quaternionic quantum computing, establishing rigorous connections between quaternion algebra, spinor representations, and the geometry of SU(2). We show that the quaternionic qubit—a unit quaternion in the Bloch sphere interpretation—provides a natural geometric encoding of two-level quantum systems, and that quaternion multiplication corresponds directly to SU(2) rotations on the Bloch sphere. We derive the quaternionic analogues of standard quantum gates, prove that any SU(2) gate can be expressed as a unit quaternion, and demonstrate that the double-cover relationship between SU(2) and SO(3) has direct implications for phase accumulation in quaternionic circuits. We further examine the quaternionic Hilbert space structure and discuss how non-commutativity of quaternion multiplication constrains the design of multi-qubit systems. This paper establishes the mathematical foundation for the subsequent papers in the RQM quaternionic quantum computing series.
papers.notes: This is the first paper in the RQM quaternionic quantum computing series. It establishes notation and conventions used in all subsequent papers.
